name: sktime
tagline: Unified scikit-learn-style toolkit for time series ML
seoTitle: sktime - Unified Time Series ML Framework in Python
metaDescription: sktime is a scikit-learn compatible Python framework for time series forecasting, classification, regression, clustering, and transformations.
overview: sktime is a Python library for machine learning with time series. It offers a single, unified interface across several time series learning tasks, so the same fit and predict pattern works whether you are forecasting a signal, classifying it, or transforming it.

The framework follows scikit-learn interface conventions and interoperates with scikit-learn, statsmodels, tsfresh, and PyOD. It builds on pandas and NumPy data structures and runs in memory on a single machine, which keeps it approachable for people who already know the scientific Python stack.

Because it treats a time series as the core data type, sktime is a practical base for physiological and biosignal work, where recordings such as heart-rate or EEG traces need to be classified, forecast, or turned into features before further analysis.
features: Unified scikit-learn-style interface across multiple time series tasks
Forecasting, classification, regression, and transformations as stable modules
Clustering, anomaly and changepoint detection, and distances or kernels as maturing modules
Composite model building with pipelines, ensembling, and tuning
Interfaces to scikit-learn, statsmodels, tsfresh, and PyOD
Extension templates for adding custom algorithms

gettingStarted.intro: Install sktime with pip, then load a series and fit a forecaster using the scikit-learn-style API.

steps.heading: Install sktime
steps.body: Install the core package from PyPI. To pull in optional dependencies for all modules, install the all_extras variant instead.

steps.heading: Install with all dependencies (optional)
steps.body: Some modules rely on extra libraries. This variant installs sktime together with those optional dependencies.

steps.heading: Fit a forecaster
steps.body: Load a sample series, split it into train and test periods, then fit a forecaster and predict over the test index using the familiar fit and predict pattern.
steps.code:
from sktime.datasets import load_airline
from sktime.forecasting.theta import ThetaForecaster
from sktime.split import temporal_train_test_split

y = load_airline()
y_train, y_test = temporal_train_test_split(y)
forecaster = ThetaForecaster(sp=12)
forecaster.fit(y_train)
y_pred = forecaster.predict(y_test.index)

useCases: Forecast future values of a time series, such as demand, sensor, or vital-sign readings
Classify physiological and biosignal recordings like ECG or EEG traces into categories
Turn raw time series into features with transformations before feeding them to a model
Build and tune composite pipelines that combine transformations, models, and validation
